intro: Hymenostephium quitensis, also known as Quitensia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Compositae (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Quitensia has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hymenostephium quitensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Quitensia is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues.
